title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues RAG is not dead despite social media discourse suggesting otherwise, citing Cursor's semantic search gains of 24% answer accuracy and 2.6% code retention, plus surging Google search volume for RAG in mid-2025. He reframes RAG as a broader retrieval paradigm combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters iteratively, not just vector search alone, and characterizes embeddings as cached compute justified by query volume.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer pushes back on the claim that RAG is dead, arguing the discourse conflates RAG with vector search alone. He reframes RAG as a broader retrieval paradigm that iteratively combines vector search, full text search, regex, and filters, and casts embeddings as cached compute whose cost is justified by query volume. Evidence cited includes Cursor's semantic search gains of 24% answer accuracy and 2.6% code retention, plus rising Google search volume for RAG in mid-2025.
